1: # Implementation Plan: 🔑 Key Center & Facebook Page Token Manager
2: 
3: This plan details the design, structure, and implementation of a dedicated "Key Center (กุญแจ & API)" Settings tab in Content Factory V2. This tab secures and manages client-side API keys, cookies, and Facebook Access Tokens in local browser storage, ensuring 100% git-safety (zero risk of credentials leaks).
4: 
5: ---
6: 
7: ## User Review Required
8: 
9: > [!IMPORTANT]
10: > **Client-Side Security Model**: All keys (OpenRouter API, Facebook User Token, Facebook Page Access Tokens) will be saved exclusively in the client's browser `localStorage`. 
11: > - **Zero Server Retention**: Keys are never transmitted to the Node.js backend or stored in SQLite database files (which could be accidentally committed to Git).
12: > - **Git-Safe & Zero-Config Portability**: When the codebase is cloned on a new machine or Git branch, the keys must be entered once locally on the new browser instance. This fully satisfies the secure isolation requirement.

5: ---

7: ## 1. ⚙️ ตั้งค่าพื้นฐาน (Basic Configurations Panel at Top)
8: - **Top Header Integration**:

10:   - **Left Column: 🏷️ ตราประทับ (Logo)**:
11:     - Button **"🔗 อัพโหลดโลโก้ใหม่"**: Custom uploader to write logo image files into SQLite / `content_vault/logos/` directory.
12:     - **Saved gallery**: Circular buttons of preloaded transparent logos (Crown, AI badge) and custom uploaded files, showing an active border when selected.
13:     - **Active indicator**: Renders active logo preview block with a `✅ ใช้งานอยู่` tag and an "เอาออก" clear button.
14:   - **Right Column: 📁 คลังรูป Stock**:
15:     - Button **"📁 เลือกคลังรูป Stock"**: Linked directly to backend macOS POSIX AppleScript dialo

68: ## 6. Latest Resolved Hotfixes 🛠️
69: 1. **Vite Primary Port Restoration**:
70:    - Detected and cleared duplicate zombie Node processes listening on port `5173` (`Google` Chrome connections and stale `node` tasks).
71:    - Re-bound the active Vite development server to **`http://localhost:5173/`** so your browser sessions automatically pull the latest V2 workspace layouts with instant hot reload (HMR).
72: 2. **TypeScript Compilation Cleanliness**:
73:    - Replaced the plain `✕` character in the basic configuration logo picker with the `<Trash2 className="w-3 h-3 text-white" />` icon, utilising the imported Lucide icon and resolving the strict `unused import 'Trash2'` warning that caused previous production build scripts to fail.
74: 3. **Corrected Logo Management Endpoints**:
75:    - Re-routed the base64 logo uploader and delete handler calls in `src/App.tsx` from `${API_BASE}/logos` to `${API_BASE}/vault/logos` to align with the Express endpoints, ensuring uploading and deleting custom logo files inside SQLite database and directories works perfectly.
76: 4. **Vanilla CSS Swatches & Color Boxes Restoration**:
77:    - Identified that the project runs entirely on vanilla CSS (defined in `index.css`) rather than full TailwindCSS framework. Since utility classes like `grid-cols-2`, `h-5`, `w-2`, `h-2`, etc. did not exist, the color blocks collapsed into thin gray lines and the grid rendered as a single vertical column.
78:    - Built custom CSS classes (`.swatches-grid`, `.swatch-card`, `.swatch-bar`, `.swatch-bar-block`, `.swatch-dot`) and integrated them directly at the end of `index.css`.
79:    - Updated the theme swatch React components in `src/App.tsx` to utilize these high-performance CSS classes. The colors and gradient bars now render inside beautiful, distinct boxes exactly as requested!

1: # Implementation Plan - Discovery Portal: Bulk Copywriting & Background Image Fixes
2: 
3: This plan details how to resolve the issues where:
4: 1. Copywriting is not generated correctly (because the backend uses a mock/default SQLite API key instead of the user's active client-side OpenRouter key).
5: 2. The generated posters use the wrong background image (because the bulk renderer leaks the global `canvasBgImage` state to all items in the queue).
6: 
7: ## Proposed Changes
8: 
9: ### Frontend Code
10: 
11: #### [MODIFY] [App.tsx](file:///Users/paulpolsulintaboon/Documents/GitHub/ContentFactory/frontend/src/App.tsx)
12: - **API Key Transmission**:
13:   - Update all calls to the `/generate-copywriting` endpoint to query the OpenRouter key from `localStorage.getItem('openrouter_key')` and pass it in the JSON body as `openrouter_key`.
14:   - Affected functions:
15:     - `handleGenerateCopywritingForItem`
16:     - `handleBulkGenerateCopywriting`
17:     - `handleRewriteCopywritingWithFeedback`
18: - **Background Image Leak Fix**:
19:   - Update the queue renderer `runNextQueueItem` to prevent using a background image from another item.
20:   - Check if `canvasBgImage` is actually present in the current queue item's `item.media_paths` list before using it as the `base_image`. If not, fall back to `item.media_paths[0]` (the item's own first image).
21: 
22: ### Backend Code
23: 
24: #### [MODIFY] [vault.ts](file:///Users/paulpolsulintaboon/Documents/GitHub/ContentFactory/backend/src/routes/vault.ts)
25: - **Backend API Key Verification**:
26:   - In `POST /api/vault/contents/:id/generate-copywriting`, read `openrouter_key` from the request body.
27:   - If a valid key is provided in the body, use it instead of reading the active key from the SQLite `api_credentials` table.
28: 
29: ## Verification Plan
30: 
31: ### Automated Tests
32: - Run `npm run build` in the `frontend` folder to verify TypeScript/Vite compilation.
33: 
34: ### Manual Verification
35: - Render a bulk batch of 2 or more selected items on the canvas control tab.
36: - Check that each generated poster uses its own respective background image from the source article/video instead of sharing the first item's image.
37: - Test generating copywriting and verify it uses the valid API key passed from the client rather than falling back to the simulated mock text.
38:

1: # Implementation Plan - Graphic Canvas Bulk AI Copywriting Logs
2: 
3: This plan adds detailed progress logging for the AI Copywriting generation flow inside the 🎨 Graphic Canvas, provides a hide/show log panel, and helps troubleshoot why AI copywriting falls back to identical/simulated text due to API issues.
4: 
5: ## Proposed Changes
6: 
7: ### Backend Route
8: 
9: #### [MODIFY] [vault.ts](file:///Users/paulpolsulintaboon/Documents/GitHub/ContentFactory/backend/src/routes/vault.ts)
10: - Update the `/contents/:id/generate-copywriting` endpoint:
11:   - If no `apiKey` is found or retrieved, explicitly set `is_simulated: true` and `error_message: 'ไม่พบ API Key สำหรับ OpenRouter'`.
12:   - In the OpenRouter/Gemini call `catch` block, capture the exact error message and store it under `is_simulated: true` and `error_message`.
13:   - When returning the JSON response to the client, include `is_simulated` and `error_message` in the data object so the client can inspect them.
14:   - Fix any potential crash if `resJson.error` is returned from OpenRouter by throwing a clear error.
15: 
16: ### Frontend App
17: 
18: #### [MODIFY] [App.tsx](file:///Users/paulpolsulintaboon/Documents/GitHub/ContentFactory/frontend/src/App.tsx)
19: - **State Declaration**:
20:   - Add `showCanvasLogs` state to control the visibility of the logs panel (defaults to `true`).
21: - **Logging Integration in Bulk Flow**:
22:   - Clear and initialize logs inside `handleBulkGenerateCopywriting`.
23:   - Log start progress, current item index, title, endpoint request payload info, and API keys metadata.
24:   - When the response is received, log if it succeeded using genuine AI or if it fell back to local simulation due to API errors (printing the exact `error_message`).
25:   - Scroll the terminal to the bottom using `scrollTerminal('canvas')`.
26: - **Logging Integration in Single Flow**:
27:   - Log similar diagnostic messages when `handleGenerateCopywritingForItem` is invoked.
28: - **UI Terminal Panel**:
29:   - Render a toggle button `ซ่อน Log` / `แสดง Log` inside the bulk copywriting card matching the premium dark glassmorphic design of ContentFactory.
30:   - Under the toggle, render a terminal-style console displaying `logs.canvas` lines with color-coded tags:
31:     - Purple for system actions `[SYSTEM]`
32:     - Cyan for active process items `[PROCESS]`
33:     - Yellow for warnings/simulated fallbacks `[WARNING]`
34:     - Green for successes `[SUCCESS]`
35:     - Red for errors `[ERROR]`
36: 
37: ## Verification Plan
38: 
39: ### Automated Tests
40: - Run build verification using `npm run build` in both `frontend` and `backend` directories.
41: 
42: ### Manual Verification
43: - Select 2 items in the Graphic Canvas list.
44: - Click `เริ่มสั่ง AI เขียนบทความและพาดหัวทั้งหมด`.
45: - Verify that the terminal log panel pops up and displays live progression.
46: - Check that toggle button hides and shows the log panel correctly.
47: - If OpenRouter key is empty or invalid, verify that the console outputs: `[WARNING] API Key ใช้งานไม่ได้หรือระบบ AI ขัดข้อง: ...` showing the user the exact cause of the identical text fallback.

1: # Walkthrough - Graphic Canvas Bulk AI Copywriting Logs
2: 
3: I have successfully resolved the lack of visibility during bulk AI copywriting generation, implemented a detailed terminal log viewer, added a hide/show log toggle button, and updated the backend to track and return API errors.
4: 
5: ## Changes Made
6: 
7: ### 1. Backend Route Updates
8: - **File Modified**: [vault.ts](file:///Users/paulpolsulintaboon/Documents/GitHub/ContentFactory/backend/src/routes/vault.ts)
9: - Updated `/api/vault/contents/:id/generate-copywriting` endpoint to prevent silent fallbacks to simulated copywriting:
10:   - If no `apiKey` is found (either client-supplied or database-stored), the endpoint now explicitly sets `is_simulated: true` and `error_message: 'ไม่พบ API Key สำหรับ OpenRouter'`.
11:   - Wrapped OpenRouter call in a robust `try-catch` block that catches exact network or response errors (such as `401 Unauthorized` or missing properties) and saves the error message under `is_simulated: true` and `error_message` inside the returned database payload.
12:   - Returned the `is_simulated` flag and `error_message` as part of the JSON response payload.
13:

1: # Implementation Plan - Graphic Canvas Image Selection Persistence
2: 
3: This plan resolves the issue where selected backdrop keyframes in the 🎨 Graphic Canvas are not persisted when switching between articles. We will save the selected image in the item's metadata and load it automatically upon selecting an article.
4: 
5: ## Proposed Changes
6: 
7: ### Frontend App
8: 
9: #### [MODIFY] [App.tsx](file:///Users/paulpolsulintaboon/Documents/GitHub/ContentFactory/frontend/src/App.tsx)
10: 
11: - **Selection Initialization**:
12:   - Update the `useEffect` hook that loads `canvasSelectedItem` details to check for a previously selected background image (`selected_bg_image` inside `canvasSelectedItem.metadata.copywriting`).
13:   - If a saved background image path exists and is in `media_paths`, set `canvasBgImage` to it. Otherwise, fall back to index `0` as before.
14: 
15: - **Auto-Save Image Selection Helper**:
16:   - Implement a new helper function `handleSelectCanvasBgImage(item, path)` that:
17:     1. Sets `canvasBgImage` state to `path`.
18:     2. Updates the item's `selected_bg_image` inside `metadata.copywriting` in the local React states (`canvasImportedItems`, `canvasAllItems`, and `canvasSelectedItem`).
19:     3. Performs a silent background `PUT` request to `/api/vault/contents/${item.id}/metadata` to update the item's metadata in the SQLite database instantly.
20: 
21: - **Update UI Actions**:
22:   - In the thumbnail click handler, replace `onClick={() => setCanvasBgImage(path)}` with `onClick={() => handleSelectCanvasBgImage(item, path)}`.
23:   - In the cycler back/forward arrow click handlers, replace `setCanvasBgImage(...)` with `handleSelectCanvasBgImage(canvasSelectedItem, ...)`.
24:   - In the `💾 บันทึกการแก้ไข` (Save Manual Edits) button handler, ensure `selected_bg_image: canvasBgImage` is included in the updated copywriting payload so it preserves the image selection if the user edits text.
25:

43:   - The CSV builder looped through the results but pulled the `headline` and `caption` from the globally selected canvas item (`canvasSelectedItem`) instead of looking up each file's corresponding source item.
44: - **Solution**:
45:   - Linked each upload result's `file_path` back to its graphic record in `contentGraphics` to obtain the `content_id`.
46:   - Found the corresponding copywriting item in the local item lists (`canvasAllItems` or `canvasImportedItems`) using the `content_id`.
47:   - Exported the unique `headline` and `caption` for each uploaded poster row in the CSV file, falling back to `canvasSelectedItem` only if the graphic record could not be mapped.
48:   - Fixed this mapping inside both Dropbox upload button callbacks.
49:
